sourceMovie.title: AVP: Alien vs. Predator
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: AVP: Alien vs. Predator works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lifeforce
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If AVP: Alien vs. Predator clicked for you, Lifeforce (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Megaboa
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If AVP: Alien vs. Predator clicked for you, Megaboa (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ultimate Avengers: The Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If AVP: Alien vs. Predator clicked for you, Ultimate Avengers: The Movie (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after AVP: Alien vs. Predator?
faq.answer: Try Lifeforce, Megaboa, Ultimate Avengers: The Movie—each shares DNA with AVP: Alien vs. Predator while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is AVP: Alien vs. Predator?
faq.answer: AVP: Alien vs. Predator (2004) is best described as Adventure, Science Fiction, Action, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is AVP: Alien vs. Predator part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "AVP Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
